calendar.matters:
Notice Of Motion And Motion For Writ Of Mandate Enforcing Public Records Act Against Respondent State Bar Of California
Off Calendar May-05-2026 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Julian Sarkar, Esq. 415-795-8795 Sarkar Law for Petitioner.
Raymond Rollan, Esq. 415-538-2000 Office of General Counsel for Respondent State Bar of California.
Court reporter: Maya Morohoshi, CSR 564, 415-305-7660, maya.morohoshi5@gmail.com

Parties state appearance. The Court announces the tentative ruling of Hearing Required; the Courts sets out reasons for an In-camera review of "attachment C-200"

Hearing is held.
Mr. Sarkar presents arguments; possible exemptions, not entitled to redaction unless cofidential.
Mr. Rollan submits on papers, does not contest the TR, has a USB and printed copy.

10:22am In-camera hearing is held in the conference room.

11:09am Court is back in session. The Court completes In-camera review of "Attachment C-200" and states its ruling on the record. (as fully reported in the reporter's notes/transcript) The Court limits redactions to the first page only and returns the original attachment to Mr. Rollan. The Court keeps a copy of page 1 with redactions.

The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

PETITIONER CHRISTINE TUMA's Motion For Writ Of Mandate Enforcing Public Records Act Against Respondent State Bar Of California.

The Court grants the motion for reasons stated on the record. Mr. Sarkar to promptly prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the Court's ruling. Disclosure is 15 days from the notice of entry of order. (as fully reported in the reporter's notes/transcript)

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court reporter: Maya Morohoshi, CSR 564, 415-305-7660, maya.morohoshi5@gmail.com, Reported. (302/JMQ)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Strike Portions Of First Amended Petition And Complaint For Injunctive Relief And Declaratory Relief For Violations Of California Public Records Act
Off Calendar Jan-19-2022 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES:

HEARING RE: RESPONDENT STATE BAR OF CALIFORNIA's Motion To Strike Portions Of First Amended Petition And Complaint For Injunctive Relief And Declaratory Relief For Violations Of California Public Records Act.

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling. Matter is off calendar. Second Amended Petition filed on January 5, 2022.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: Sean Kane; Not Reported (302/EPS)

DEMURRER
Off Calendar Jul-23-2020 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE:
Julian Sarkar (415-795-8795; jsarkar@sarkar.law) for Petitioner, Christine Tuma.
Caroline Holmes (415-538-2186) Office of General Counsel for Respondeny, The State Bar of California

ARGUED (as fully reported in the reporter's transcript). The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

RESPONDENT STATE BAR OF CALIFORNIA's DEMURRER to the complaint for injunctive and declaratory relief is sustained without leave to amend. Plaintiff's opposition verges on the frivolous.

The demurrer to the first cause of action for violation of the Bagley-Keene Open Meeting Act is sustained without leave to amend. The closed session of the May 28, 2020 meeting of a Subcommittee of the Committee of Bar Examiners concerning the grading of an individual's California bar examination that is the subject of the complaint is exempt from the open meeting requirements of the Bagley-Keene Act. As the Court explained in its May 28, 2020 order denying plaintiff's application for a TRO and OSC re preliminary injunction, the Act shall not be construed to "[p]revent state bodies that administer the licensing of persons engaging in businesses or professions from holding closed sessions to prepare, approve, grade or administer examinations." (Gov. Code 11126(c)(1); see also Bus. & Profs. Code 6026.7(c)(3) ["a closed session may be held for those meetings, or portions thereof, relating to . . . [t]he preparation, approval, grading, or administration of the California Bar Examination or the First-Year Law Students' Examination."].)

The demurrer to the second cause of action for violation of the California Public Records Act is also sustained without leave to amend. Plaintiff has not filed a verified petition, as expressly required by that Act. (Gov. Code 6259(a).) In any event, the attachment prepared for the Subcommittee's use at the closed meeting is exempt from disclosure under several statutory exemptions, including Government Code section 6254(c) [where disclosure would constitute "an unwarranted invasion of personal privacy"], section 6265(g) [licensing examination data], and Business & Professions Code section 6060.25(a) [confidential applicant data].) Plaintiff fails to respond to any of these arguments.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman, Clerk: S. Le, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. =(302/EPS)
